rationale_top3: Cleanliness (13/15) — Consistently praised for 'spotlessly clean' amenities and 'cleanest ablution blocks' with beautifully maintained grounds, though occasional reports of ants and mould in older cabins. Nature (14/20) — Features beautiful gardens, abundant trees, and native wildlife including Quenda bandicoots, though situated in suburban setting near busy roads and airport rather than natural bushland. Entertainment (11/20) — Provides nice playground and 'fantastic' pool appreciated by families, but limited by early closing times (pool 7pm, camp kitchen 9pm) and lack of waterpark, jumping pillow or structured kids programs.
rationale_honourable: Banksia Tourist Park offers a well-maintained base for families exploring Perth, with consistently praised clean amenities, beautiful gardens, and a good swimming pool. While lacking dedicated kids entertainment facilities and located away from major attractions, the park provides reliable infrastructure, pet-friendly accommodation options, and spacious powered sites that suit families needing a quiet, affordable stopover with good access to Perth's northern and southern routes.
kids_play: Basic playground, limited structured kids activities
pet_detail: Pet friendly cabins available with restrictions on large/certain breeds; typically small to medium dogs only; additional daily fee applies.
best_for: Budget-conscious families with young children needing a clean, quiet overnight stop with pool access while touring Perth, especially those travelling with small pets.
executive_summary:
PARK: Banksia Tourist Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 243

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (11/20) — Reviews consistently mention a "nice playground" and "great activity park for the young ones" but no waterpark, jumping pillow, games room access after 5:30pm, or structured kids programs. Families noted the pool as "a big bonus" and "fantastic" but it closes at 7pm and the camp kitchen locks at 9pm, limiting evening family activities.

Nature (14/20) — The park features "beautiful gardens," "lots of trees," abundant birdlife, and native Quenda (bandicoots) that guests loved spotting. However, it's suburban rather than bushland, located near busy roads and airport, with no beach, creek, or natural swimming holes. Reviews describe "bushy" surrounds but emphasize it's a manicured caravan park rather than a natural setting.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Strong praise for "spotlessly clean" amenities, "very clean and well maintained" facilities, and "cleanest ablution blocks we have seen in our travels." Some complaints about occasional ants, mould in older cabins, and toilets needing more frequent cleaning. Overall maintenance scored well with "beautifully maintained" grounds.

Value (10/15) — Mixed feedback with some noting "great value for money" and "super affordable price" while others complained about "$175 for one night," "overpriced charge," "very pricey for what it was," and extra charges for toilet paper, dogs, and limited wifi. Budget families appreciated it; those expecting resort amenities felt disappointed.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviews mention "nice big sites," "large concrete sites," and "spacious" caravan sites, but many noted "internal sites can be tight for vans," "tiny parking space," "tight and poorly designed," and difficulty reversing into sites. Large rigs struggled with access and permanent residents blocking lanes.

Sentiment (7/10) — Positive repeat visitors and recommendations balanced by significant complaints about rude reception staff, inflexible refund policies, discriminatory pet policies, and feeling "scammed." Many said "will definitely be back" while others stated "never again" and "won't be recommending."

Location (6/10) — Praised for being "close to Midland," "30-40 minutes north or south of city," "10 mins from airport," with bus stop at gate and train access nearby. However, "far from shops," "not very convenient without a car," constant road noise from Roe Freeway, and low-flying aircraft. Good for transit, not for walking to attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
1. No dedicated kids entertainment facilities - no waterpark, no kids club, no jumping pillow, limited playground equipment, and facilities close early (pool at 7pm, camp kitchen at 9pm) preventing evening family activities.
2. Suburban location with significant traffic and aircraft noise, no beach/creek access, and too far from attractions to walk - families need a car and use it primarily as a transit base rather than a destination.
3. Inconsistent service standards with multiple reviews citing "rude reception staff," "aggressive confrontation," inflexible policies even for family emergencies, extra charges for basic amenities, and discriminatory treatment regarding pets and vehicle types.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"beautiful gardens very well kept", "spotlessly clean amenities", "Nice BBQ and playground", "pool was a bonus", "pet friendly cabins", "quiet peaceful safe place", "lots of permanent residents", "rude reception staff", "way too many dogs barking", "great location easy access to Midland", "not much that you can do", "excellent value for money", "well maintained park"

rationale_top3: Excellent strategic location just 5-10 minutes from Perth Airport with easy access to Swan Valley wineries, shopping centres, and Perth attractions, making it an ideal base for exploring the region Modern, well-maintained facilities with recently renovated amenity blocks described as 'best amenity blocks in WAs van parks' and 'spotless', complemented by genuinely friendly and helpful staff Spacious caravan sites with good concrete slabs and drive-through options, plus pet-friendly accommodation in a leafy setting with nearby paddocks and river parks for dog walking
rationale_honourable: Discovery Holiday Park Perth Airport earns Silver recognition primarily for its excellent location near the Swan Valley, modern amenities including renovated bathroom blocks, and genuinely helpful staff who go above and beyond. Families appreciate the spacious sites for caravans, clean pool area, and proximity to wineries, shopping centres, and Perth attractions. While the park has strong fundamentals including pet-friendly cabins and good camp kitchens, recurring issues with security/theft, inconsistent cleanliness in some accommodations, poor WiFi connectivity, and limited entertainment facilities prevent it from reaching Gold status.
water_fun: Small pool, clean but often closed
kids_play: Playground present, kids enjoyed, limited features
pet_detail: Pet friendly cabins and sites available with dogs allowed on leash
best_for: This park best suits families with young children staying in cabins who need proximity to Perth Airport or Swan Valley attractions for short visits, rather than those seeking a destination holiday park experience.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Holiday Park Perth (Swan Valley)
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-05-28
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 354

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with a small pool that families mention is "lovely and clean" but "on the smaller side" and frequently closed for maintenance. Kids enjoyed the basic playground and some mention a games room and theatre room, but many families noted "not much to do like other parks not child friendly as much" with minimal organized activities or dedicated kids' entertainment areas.

Nature (12/20) — Park situated in Swan Valley wine region with vineyard views from some sites and "surrounded by lovely trees and greenery" creating a "serene environment." Guests appreciate being "close to the beautiful Perth Hills" and nearby Whiteman Park, though the park itself is more suburban than bushland with limited natural exploration opportunities for children.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Highly inconsistent with modern amenities praised as "spotless" and "the best amenities blocks in WAs van parks," but multiple reports of "cabin not cleaned between guests floor was filthy," "ants everywhere," "mould in the shower," camp kitchen "disgustingly dirty bins not emptied," and "toilet paper not replaced for 2 days." Major variance between new and older facilities.

Value (9/15) — Mixed value perception with many finding it "pretty average for the price" and "EXPENSIVE" at $60-70/night for basic sites. Families report "$260 per night" cabins, "almost $900 for three nights," and "not worth the price they charge." Some appreciated "reasonable prices" for location but overall sentiment suggests overpriced for facilities and experience provided.

Site Size (6/10) — Caravan sites described as "very small" with families "unable to pull out awning," "too small for 20ft caravan," "dense layout," and tent sites "super small" on "hard rock dirt." Positive mentions of "great size sites" and "spacious sites" mainly for drive-through and ensuite options, but unpowered and standard sites consistently criticized as cramped.

Sentiment (7/10) — Polarized feedback with loyal repeat visitors praising staff and amenities, but significant negative experiences around theft ("$5000 worth of stuff stolen," "had food stolen from fridge," "stuff stolen of front porch"), safety concerns, and disappointment leading many to state "wouldn't stay here again," "will not be returning," and "would never stay there again." Recommendation rate heavily impacted by security issues.

Location (9/10) — Excellent location "right next to the swan valley," "close to airport," "surrounded by wineries," with easy access to shopping at DFO, Midland Gate, and attractions. Convenient for "fly in fly out days" and exploring Perth, though noise from "planes taking off," "trains with horns blaring," and busy road noted by some families.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Serious and repeated security/theft issues with multiple families reporting stolen belongings, management showing "complete lack of responsibility," no security cameras in key areas, and police confirming "ongoing issues with theft" creating an unsafe environment for family holidays.

2. Severe inconsistency in accommodation quality and cleanliness, with modern cabins praised but numerous reports of ant infestations, uncleaned units, broken fixtures, flooding bathrooms, maintenance neglect, and tent sites described as unsuitable with facilities "over 150m away" and "NO TOILET."

3. Inadequate family entertainment and facilities for a holiday park, with a small pool frequently closed, minimal kids' activities, poor WiFi connectivity throughout ("only works 5m from office"), and a focus on cabin accommodation over caravan/camping facilities leading many families to find it "not child friendly as much" compared to other Discovery Parks.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved pool and playground", "theft clearly a known and ongoing issue", "amenities spotless and new", "WiFi terrible barely have any signal", "staff are very friendly and helpful", "great location close to Swan Valley", "cabin riddled with ants", "tent sites NO TOILET no shower", "wouldn't stay here again", "unsafe rampant theft", "beautiful park well managed", "horrible park ever LOTS OF CRIMES"

rationale_top3: Entertainment (12/20): Small clean playground on-site with adjacent external facilities including Wonder World soft play centre, Air Thrill, and mini golf, though entertainment is primarily off-site rather than integrated park amenities Cleanliness (11/15): Highly inconsistent standards with recent reviews showing deteriorating conditions including uncut grass, disgusting shower facilities, and inadequate waste management, despite some families praising spotlessly clean facilities Value (10/15): Reasonable pricing for families seeking economical stopover accommodation, though surcharges for electricity and expensive laundry facilities balanced the value perception
rationale_honourable: Perth Caravan Park offers families a functional stopover with clean facilities and a small playground that children can enjoy. The site has undergone recent renovations and benefits from proximity to external entertainment options like Wonder World soft play and mini golf next door. While it serves well as a transit park with helpful staff and basic amenities, the location next to the A9 dual carriageway, inconsistent maintenance standards, and limited natural environment prevent it from achieving a higher ranking for Australian family holiday standards.
water_fun: No water facilities mentioned in reviews
kids_play: Clean playground on-site, soft play nearby
pet_detail: Dog park available next door (booking required), dogs allowed on site
best_for: Budget-conscious families with young children wanting a clean, affordable base to access Perth city attractions and nearby soft play centres.
executive_summary:
PARK: Perth Caravan Park & Camp Site
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 42

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews highlight an on-site playground described as "clean and safe" and "one of the best in scottish sites," with exceptional off-site options including Wonder World soft play centre, Air Thrill, mini golf, and a golf driving range all within walking distance. However, on-site entertainment is limited to the playground, requiring families to leave the park for activities.

Nature (11/20) — Guests appreciate watching "lots of wild rabbits" and access to a dog park next door with "lots of walks for friendly dogs." The location lacks significant natural beauty, being situated on an industrial estate "under electrical pylons" near the A9, though some found it "quiet" with green spaces for football.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Recent reviews praise cleanliness with "spotlessly clean and tidy" facilities and "lovely clean toilets and showers," especially post-renovation. However, multiple 2024 reviews cite "grass not cut," "uneven and potted pathways," and one extreme complaint about "disgusting" dishwashing facilities with bins not emptied, showing inconsistent maintenance standards.

Value (12/15) — Families describe it as "amazing value" with "great price" for basic camping, though one review noted "high surcharge for electric connection." Most consider it good value as a stopover, though the limited on-site amenities and occasional maintenance issues affect perceived value.

Site Size (7/10) — Reviewers report "well spaced" pitches with an "8m unit easy to get in and out" and "nice open space." Negative reviews mention "tiny untidy pitch" and crowded conditions during peak times with units "touching" each other, suggesting inconsistent pitch allocation.

Sentiment (4/10) — Sentiment is deeply polarized: enthusiastic families call it a "favorite site" they'll return to, while others declare "WONT BE BACK" and "worst campsite yet." Three 1-star reviews in mid-2024 cite serious issues with travellers on site making families "not feel comfortable," significantly impacting family sentiment. Recent 2026 reviews show improvement.

Location (3/10) — The park's proximity to the A9 creates noise issues, with multiple reviews stating "difficult to sleep" and "motorway noise" though some noted "no noise at all." Bus access to Perth city centre is convenient ("15 minutes away"), but the industrial estate setting lacks appeal for destination holidays.

WHY NOT 100:
The park lost significant points for inconsistent maintenance and cleanliness standards, with reviews ranging from "spotlessly clean" to "grass not cut" and "disgusting" dishwashing facilities within the same year. Location next to the A9 dual carriageway creates noise disturbances that multiple families found disruptive to sleep. The presence of travellers housed by council in mid-2024 created safety concerns that made multiple family reviewers explicitly state they "didn't feel comfortable" and wouldn't return.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"one of the best and cleanest we've seen in scottish sites", "wonder world big soft play center", "Not good for dogs or kids", "worst campsite yet", "didn't feel comfortable", "amazing value", "grass not cut, tiny untidy pitch", "spotlessly clean and tidy"

rationale_top3: Nature & surroundings (12/15) - The park benefits from its Swan Valley location, offering reasonable natural scenery and outdoor appeal Cleanliness standards (9/15) - Facilities maintain good hygiene standards with acceptable upkeep across accommodation and amenity areas Entertainment facilities (8/15) - Basic recreational amenities available though not extensive, providing some activity options for guests
rationale_honourable: Location convenience (8/15) - Situated in Swan Valley with access to Perth region attractions and wineries Value for money (8/15) - Pricing appears reasonable relative to facilities and experience offered Overall guest sentiment (7/15) - Generally positive reception with some reservations about specific aspects

rationale_honourable: BIG4 Perth Midland Tourist Park offers a practical, budget-friendly stopover location with convenient access to Midland Gate shopping centre and Swan Valley. While the park shows its age with dated facilities and a significant permanent resident population, families appreciate the spotlessly clean amenities, helpful staff, and good value pricing. The central location makes it ideal for exploring Perth metro attractions, though families should manage expectations around entertainment facilities and site presentation.

executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Perth Midland Tourist Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-06-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 191

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with reviews mentioning a games room with air hockey, table tennis, and DVD library, but equipment often damaged or outdated. Pool and spa frequently reported as unheated or freezing cold despite heated pool claims. Activity facilities minimal for families with young children, with one family noting damaged air hockey table and poor table tennis bats.

Nature (6/20) — Urban caravan park setting with limited natural environment. Some cabins overlook bushland or trees providing pleasant views, but overall described as "very much a city caravan park" with minimal nature access. Close to Swan Valley wine region but not immersed in natural surroundings. Blue gum trees mentioned as dropping debris on caravans.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Amenities consistently praised as "spotlessly clean," "very clean and tidy," and "super clean." Camp kitchen, laundry, and bathroom blocks maintained to high standards. However, multiple reviews report dirty rooms with food on dishes, dusty floors, mouldy cabins, and general rundown appearance of permanent resident sites creating untidy park presentation.

Value (10/15) — Mixed feedback with some praising affordability and reasonable pricing ($118/night school holidays noted as good value), while others felt overpriced for condition. Budget accommodation adequate for stopovers. Washing machines expensive at $7 per load plus card fees. Several families felt value didn't match aging facilities and cramped sites.

Site Size (5/10) — Consistently criticized as "very small," "too small for van," and "jammed in like sardines." Multiple reviews report sites so tight visitors couldn't put out awnings or needed multiple-point turns. Concrete pads often unlevel requiring ramps. Space between sites minimal with no privacy. Permanent residents encroaching on visitor sites with trailers and quad bikes.

Sentiment (7/10) — Polarized experiences with loyal repeat visitors praising staff and location, but numerous families reporting disappointing stays and vowing not to return. Many describe feeling unsafe, uncomfortable around permanent residents, and frustrated by poor customer service. Staff generally praised as friendly and helpful, though management received criticism for lack of compassion and communication failures.

Location (11/10) — Strongest scoring category with excellent access to Midland Gate shopping centre (10-15 minute walk), Swan Valley (8 minutes drive), Perth CBD (20-30 minutes), and Perth Airport. Convenient public transport with bus stops nearby and train station walkable. Central to Perth metro activities making it ideal for event attendance and day trips.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant presence of permanent residents creates an untidy park appearance with rundown sites, junk, and visitors feeling uncomfortable or stared at, detracting from holiday park atmosphere particularly for families with children.
2. Minimal kids entertainment and recreational facilities with broken equipment, unheated freezing pool despite advertising, and very limited play areas that don't meet family holiday park expectations.
3. Very small, cramped caravan sites that are difficult to access, often unlevel, with permanent residents blocking access and encroaching on visitor spaces, plus major cleanliness issues in some cabins with dirty dishes, floors, and maintenance failures like flooding showers and broken appliances.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean amenities", "very friendly and helpful staff", "Classic Big4 older campground", "many permanent residents very untidy", "pool is FREEZING", "very small van spaces jammed in like sardines", "great camp kitchen", "close to Swan Valley and Midland Gate", "room was disgusting very dirty", "wouldn't feel comfortable leaving children unattended", "great location central to everywhere", "worst Big4 I have ever been to"

rationale_top3: Security concerns dominate recent reviews with multiple documented theft incidents from vehicles and sites, with management showing poor accountability and insufficient security measures despite repeated incidents. Urban airport location lacks natural environment, beach access, and holiday atmosphere that families seek, functioning more as convenient stopover accommodation than a holiday destination. Limited entertainment offerings beyond basic pool and games room—no waterpark, organized activities, or dedicated kids club that characterizes top-tier family holiday parks.
rationale_honourable: Discovery Parks Perth Airport offers modern, clean facilities with recently renovated amenities that consistently impress guests. The park features a pool, kids games room, and theatre room that families appreciate, along with spacious sites and friendly staff who go the extra mile. While the location is convenient for airport access and shopping, serious security concerns involving multiple thefts significantly impact the overall family holiday experience.
kids_play: Games room, theatre room, playground available

executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Perth Airport
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 133

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews frequently mention pool, playground, kids games room, and theatre room. Families appreciated Halloween activities including "haunted villa for the kids," activity packs for travelling children, and "free icy pole and kit kat for kids." However, entertainment offerings are basic compared to dedicated holiday parks.

Nature (8/20) — Described as having "lovely trees and greenery" with "leafy sites" and located near a waterway. Guests noted "lots of birds in the trees" but this is primarily an urban park near the airport, not a nature-based destination. Limited natural environment beyond landscaping.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Consistently praised with "spotless" amenities, "immaculate" cabins, and "best amenities blocks in WAs van parks." Multiple reviews highlight "very clean," "modern and clean" facilities. Some concerns about mould in bathrooms and occasional maintenance lapses with "grounds very dirty and lots of sand."

Value (9/15) — Mixed feedback with "good value for money" and "competitive price" alongside complaints of "EXPENSIVE" at "$260 per night" and "Pritty average for the price." Families felt pricing was reasonable for short stays but questioned value for extended periods.

Site Size (8/10) — Reviews mention "great size for van, annex & car," "spacious sites," and "very generous size" drive-through sites. Some guests struggled with tighter sites noting difficulty getting caravans into certain spots, requiring site changes.

Sentiment (4/10) — Severely impacted by multiple theft incidents. Reviews detail "all my valuables stolen," "LOTS OF CRIMES," "stuff stolen of the front porch," and "robbery seems far too common." Management response criticized as showing "zero responsibility." Despite positive staff interactions, security concerns dominate recent family reviews.

Location (4/10) — Convenient for airport access ("stone throw away") and shopping (DFO, Costco, Woolworths nearby). However, urban location lacks holiday appeal, with airport proximity not ideal for family holidays. "Close to city" but not near beaches or natural attractions families seek.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Rampant security issues with multiple documented thefts from vehicles and sites throughout the park, with management showing poor accountability and no meaningful security measures despite repeated incidents.
2. Urban airport location lacks the natural environment, beach access, and holiday atmosphere that families with young children prioritize for memorable holiday experiences.
3. Limited on-site entertainment beyond basic pool and games room—no waterpark, organized activities, or dedicated kids club that defines top-tier family holiday parks.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved pool and playground," "huge kids room and theatre room," "haunted villa for the kids to explore," "family friendly had an amazing Christmas," "free icy pole and kit kat for kids," "stuff stolen," "LOTS OF CRIMES," "Totally unsafe," "spotless amenities," "immaculate cabins," "EXPENSIVE," "zero responsibility"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location with exceptional beach access - families consistently praised the short walk to beautiful Coogee Beach, proximity to the Omeo Wreck for snorkelling, and convenient access to Perth and Fremantle attractions Significant security concerns with ongoing theft issues - multiple families reported bikes and e-bikes stolen daily, prevalent night-time theft, and inadequate fencing creating an unsafe environment Complete absence of family entertainment facilities - no swimming pool, no playground, no kids' activities or organized entertainment, which families specifically identified as deal-breakers for a holiday park experience
rationale_honourable: Inconsistent cleanliness standards between accommodation types (caravan site amenities generally well-maintained while cabins reported as dirty, dated and broken), and poor value for money with premium pricing not reflecting the quality of dated facilities and lack of amenities
water_fun: No pool; beach access only via walk

executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Coogee Beach
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 129

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Multiple families specifically noted "lacking amenities and activities for the kids", "no activities or swimming pools at the park for children", and "no park for kids". The park has communal areas with couches, TV and a pool table for adults, but virtually no organized kids' entertainment or facilities. One review mentioned a "facility block between the whole campsite is a little bit of a Letdown". The nearby public playground was mentioned as an asset but is outside the park itself.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional beachfront location with consistent praise: "short walk to the beach", "close walk to the beach", "fantastic beach for children in the morning", and "short stroll to the beach". Families loved the "Omeo Wreck just a tiny walk north for snorkelling", beach tracks, natural bird life, shady trees throughout the park, and the "heart lifting vista of white shore and sparkling, aqua water". The natural coastal setting is the park's strongest feature.

Cleanliness (9/15) — Mixed feedback with significant concerns. Positive reviews noted "spotless amenities", "very clean facilities", "amenities well maintained & cleaned regularly", and "scrupulously clean" ablution blocks. However, multiple families reported serious issues: "cabin was dirty and broken", "extremely poor room cleanliness", "cobwebs in the toilet, and dirt on the floor", "disgusting smell", "portacot we were given was dirty and had holes", "dirty socks" under beds, and "amenity block was left in a disgusting state". Cleanliness appears inconsistent between caravan sites (better) and cabins (problematic).

Value (8/15) — Significant concerns about value for money. Families reported "cabins are over priced and dated", "$315 per night" that "did not reflect the quality", "extra $20 per child per night, which I thought was over priced considering there are no activities", "over priced at $140/night but in a normal world only worth about $80/night", and "prices have skyrocketed over the last 3 years". Several reviews noted the location was cheaper than alternatives "but there is a good reason for that". Caravan/powered sites received better value ratings than cabins.

Site Size (6/10) — Concerning feedback about site accessibility and size. Reviews mentioned "very tight checkerboard caravan sight", "tight sites with large overhanging trees", "some sites are very difficult to get into especially with a larger van", "required reversing the length of the cul de sac to get in", and "worst caravan site". Some travelers on concrete slabs reported "spacious" and "good size" sites with shade, but the overall layout received criticism for being poorly planned for larger rigs.

Sentiment (6/10) — Polarized sentiment with location driving positive reviews but serious concerns dampening enthusiasm. Multiple families stated they "wont go back", "wouldn't stay again", "surely we won't come back", and "DO NOT STAY HERE". However, loyal guests also said "we keep coming back all the way from Victoria", "second time here absolutely love it", and "will definitely be back". Major issues included prevalent theft ("bikes stolen daily", "E-bikes stolen on a daily basis", "GANGSTERS PARADISE"), safety concerns, aggressive management behavior, and dated facilities. Staff received mixed reviews—many praised individuals like James, Carl, and Wayne, while others found management unhelpful.

Location (10/10) — Outstanding location scores with universal praise. Families loved being "right on the beach", "close to Perth and Fremantle", "10 minutes drive to Freo", "convenient for Rottnest Island day trips", "20 minutes walk to the supermarket (woolworths)", "bus stop to Fremantle was right out front", and "15min drive to Fremantle". The beach access, proximity to cafes, shopping, and Perth attractions while being "out of the hustle and bustle" was consistently highlighted as the park's premier asset.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities—no pool, no playground, no kids' activities, no waterpark—which multiple families specifically called out as deal-breakers for a holiday park experience.
2. Serious and ongoing security issues with "bikes stolen daily", "prevalent night time theft", inadequate fencing, and multiple families reporting stolen belongings, creating an unsafe environment particularly concerning for families with children.
3. Significant cleanliness and maintenance problems in cabin accommodation with reports of dirty, dated, broken facilities, spider infestations, and poor upkeep that doesn't justify premium pricing, though caravan site amenities fared better.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"lacking amenities and activities for the kids", "bikes stolen daily", "no activities or swimming pools at the park for children", "short walk to the beach", "cabin was dirty and broken", "spotless amenities", "fantastic beach for children", "prevalent night time theft", "best location next to the beach", "DO NOT STAY HERE", "great location", "old run down not worth the money"

rationale_top3: Nature and surroundings scored 12/20, indicating the natural setting and environmental appeal is the strongest aspect of this park Cleanliness achieved 11/20, suggesting acceptable but not exceptional standards in amenities and facility maintenance Value for money received 7/10, showing moderate satisfaction with pricing relative to offerings
rationale_honourable: Location accessibility scored 6/10, providing reasonable convenience for visitors to Perth WA

kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment mentioned
pet_detail: Dogs allowed with dedicated dog run area

executive_summary:
PARK: Crystal Brook Caravan Park
SCORE: 42/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-06-17
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 66

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (3/20) — Reviews explicitly state "Not kid Friendly" with "nothing flash or any extra amenities available." Multiple families note the complete absence of playground equipment, kids' club, or organized activities. One detailed review specifically warns families that "there might be better options when you consider...park facilities for kids."

Nature (12/20) — Several reviews praise natural elements with mentions of "kookaburras wake us up...Roos hang round at night" and the park being "at the foot of hills walking trails just a short 10min drive away." However, multiple reviews criticize the lack of green space, stating "more grass more trees needed" and sites described as "concrete pad."

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews showing decline. While many older reviews praise "very clean," "toilets and showers cleaned 2 times a day," and "spotless" facilities, multiple 2025-2026 reviews report deterioration under new management: "ablution block was unclean and dirty," "cabin we stayed in had mould," and facilities not cleaned for "over 24h" during water issues.

Value (6/15) — Consistent complaints about pricing with reviews stating "expensive for a concrete pad," "quite expensive for what it is," "way too overpriced for what it was," and "for 49$..it's a HUGE NO from me." Several families recommend paying slightly more for Big4 parks with better amenities.

Site Size (4/10) — Significant concerns raised with "unpowered sites were 4 very cramped spots in the corner," "Too Cramped for my Liking," and "no space for anything." One review mentions "huge" powered sites, but the majority of family-accessible sites are described as inadequate with one family "located right next to Dump Point."

Sentiment (3/10) — Overwhelmingly negative family sentiment in recent reviews. Multiple families report feeling "literally unsafe," being "kicked...to the street" with a "5month old daughter," and experiencing "horrible and disrespectful" management. Reviews warn "I don't recommend this park at all" and describe "bad energy." The park is "97% permanent residents" creating an unwelcoming environment for holidaying families.

Location (7/10) — Good proximity advantages with "close to Perth," "7km Perth Airport," and "close to city." Shopping is "ten minutes away" and there's access to Perth hills attractions. However, "no retail services within walking distance" limits convenience for families.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Completely unsuitable for families with young children - explicitly labeled "Not kid Friendly" with zero entertainment facilities, no playground, no kids' activities, and multiple families reporting unsafe feelings and unwelcoming atmosphere from predominantly permanent residents.
2. Severe site cramping and space issues - families describe "cramped spots in the corner," concrete pads with inadequate grass/trees, sites located next to dump points, and "no space for anything," making it impractical for family caravans and outdoor play.
3. Significant management and safety concerns - multiple recent reviews detail families being evicted with babies, "horrible and disrespectful" office staff, feeling "literally unsafe" with residents approaching about car contents, bond money withheld, mould in cabins, and a notable decline since 2024 management change.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Not kid Friendly," "97% of the park living there," "felt literally unsafe during our stay," "kicked myself and my 5month old daughter to the street," "cramped spots in the corner," "concrete pad...more grass more trees needed," "nothing flash or any extra amenities available," "office staff are horrible and disrespectful"

rationale_top3: Nature & Surroundings (12/20): Limited natural appeal or green space noted in guest feedback Location & Accessibility (8/20): Moderate convenience for accessing Perth city and surroundings Value for Money (6/20): Pricing does not align well with facilities and experience provided
rationale_honourable: Cleanliness Standards (5/20): Basic maintenance and hygiene levels require improvement
water_fun: Small pool only - basic but functional, though multiple reviews note it is inadequate for children's entertainment
kids_play: No playground or kids activities - reviews specifically note the absence of entertainment options for children
pet_detail: Pet friendly cabins and sites available
executive_summary:
PARK: Perth Central Caravan Park
SCORE: 42/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 119

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (3/20) — Reviews consistently note there is no playground and "definitely need some things to do for small children besides a pool." The small pool is the only entertainment facility mentioned, with multiple families stating it's inadequate for children's entertainment.

Nature (12/20) — The park benefits from proximity to Swan River with "beautiful little river/bush walk just a hop skip & a jump away" and "surrounded by nature which makes it feel like you're in a rural area." However, immediate surroundings include highway proximity and construction, limiting the natural environment appeal.

Cleanliness (6/15) — Major concerns dominate reviews: "black mould everywhere and even in the air con," "showers & toilets are stuck in the 70s," "bathrooms are filthy and some of the shower heads are broken off," "shower floors have tiles missing," and "the worst i have seen during my 6 weeks of camping trip." While some positive comments exist about cabin cleanliness, amenity blocks receive overwhelming negative feedback.

Value (5/15) — Families consistently report poor value: "most expensive of our stays," "Not worth $270 a night for a family," "expensive in comparison to other parks considering the condition," and "too expensive for very little." The pricing doesn't match facility quality according to family reviewers.

Site Size (4/10) — Reviews repeatedly mention "small spaces," "ridiculously small allocated sites," "very tight to reverse into," "sites are very very small and so hard to get vans in and out," and "parking spots tight to get into." Tree roots and obstacles within sites create additional challenges.

Sentiment (5/10) — Families express significant safety concerns: "very unsafe feel about it," "definitely do not bring families here," "my sister felt so scared at night," and "DO NOT STAY HERE." While some long-term residents and airport users are satisfied, family traveller sentiment is predominantly negative with strong warnings to avoid.

Location (7/10) — Strong proximity to airport ("5 min to airport") and city access via public transport. Close to Woolworths, petrol stations, and Swan River. However, highway noise and urban setting detract from holiday atmosphere.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness and maintenance issues in amenity blocks with widespread reports of mould, broken fixtures, missing tiles, and facilities described as "the worst" and "stuck in the 70s" that are unsuitable for families.
2. Complete absence of children's entertainment facilities beyond a small pool, with no playground, activities, or kids' club, making it inappropriate for family holidays with young children.
3. Safety and security concerns raised by multiple families citing an "unsafe feel," no gates or security, permanent residents creating uncomfortable atmosphere, and sites described as a "trailer park" rather than family holiday park.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"DO NOT STAY HERE", "worst i have seen during my 6 weeks of camping trip", "black mould everywhere and even in the air con", "trailer park not a caravan park for holiday makers", "very unsafe feel about it", "definitely do not bring families here", "ridiculously small allocated sites", "Not worth $270 a night for a family"

rationale_top3: Critical safety and cleanliness failures with multiple families reporting feeling unsafe, unusable facilities, broken locks, and disgusting bathrooms that are fundamentally unsuitable for young children Non-functional children's amenities with playground flooded and not working, pool frequently closed or dirty, and no organized kids entertainment or activities for family holidays Severely deteriorated maintenance across the park with old broken facilities, no hot water incidents, leaking taps, cracked slabs, long grass, spider webs, mould, and infrastructure described as falling apart requiring major investment
rationale_honourable: Excellent Swan Valley location consistently praised as perfect spot for wineries, vineyards and attractions, with natural environment featuring visiting kangaroos and kookaburras
water_fun: Pool present but often closed or poorly maintained
kids_play: Kids playground flooded and not functioning properly
pet_detail: Pet friendly, dogs allowed on site
best_for: Adult couples or solo travellers on a tight budget who prioritize location over facilities and plan very short stays while visiting Swan Valley wineries
executive_summary:
PARK: Acclaim Swan Valley Tourist Park
SCORE: 38/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 80

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently report the kids playground as "flooded and not functioning", pool "closed" during hot weather, or "poorly maintained". One positive noted "games room with pool table and darts" and a 6-year-old loved watching planes, but multiple families specifically warned "not somewhere you'd want to bring your family" due to lack of functioning facilities.

Nature (14/20) — The park is located in the scenic Swan Valley wine region with reviews praising "great nature around", "kangaroos come to say hello at dusk", "sweet song of the Kookaburra at sunrise", and "unpowered sites got lots of shade". The natural surroundings and proximity to vineyards, honey farms, and local markets provide good nature access, though the park itself is aging.

Cleanliness (3/15) — This is a critical weakness with overwhelming evidence: "toilets disgusting", "missing and cracked tiles, holes in walls, smell of fish/blocked drains", "bathroom ceiling full of mould", "spider webs all over", "long grass", "amenities old and falling apart", "deplorable sites", "filthy", "didn't even dare to use amenities", and "cleaner only has 20mins to clean the toilet".

Value (7/15) — Mixed feedback with some praising "affordable", "good price", "35$ unpowered and 45$ powered one of the cheapest around Perth", and "great value". However, many felt it was "overpriced" at "$45 a night. Rip off!", "$85 for 1 night" considered excessive given the poor condition, with several stating they found better accommodation elsewhere for $60.

Site Size (6/10) — Reviews indicate "large enough to have 2 vehicles parked", "plenty room for van car and awning", and "concrete & grass sites". However, multiple warnings about "sites cramped and very hard to get into", "tight for longer vans", "bad layout", roads with no turnaround requiring reversing, and "massive potholes" in the driveway.

Sentiment (3/10) — Predominantly negative family sentiment with serious safety concerns: "did not feel safe", "felt very unsafe", "constantly worried about belongings", "undesirable people", reports of domestic violence incidents, drug use, and "mentally ill man shouting and banging". Several families left within minutes or hours. A few long-term residents and budget travellers had positive experiences, but family recommendation rate is very low.

Location (9/10) — The strongest aspect with consistent praise: "great location", "fabulous location", "perfect spot", "awesome location right in the centre of all the action that the Swan Valley offers", "close to all the great vineyards", "ideal situation for visiting Swan Valley", "nearby Perth and Perth airport (15mn drive to both)", convenient to attractions and wineries.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Critical cleanliness and maintenance failures with disgusting bathroom facilities, broken fixtures, mould, spider webs, and overwhelmingly negative hygiene reports that make this unsuitable for families with young children.
2. Non-functioning or closed children's facilities including flooded playground and pool frequently closed during hot weather when families need it most, with no reliable kids entertainment infrastructure.
3. Serious safety concerns reported by multiple families including permanent residents with concerning behavior, noise at all hours, drug use, domestic violence incidents, and families feeling unsafe enough to leave within hours of arrival.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"not somewhere you'd want to bring your family", "kids park flooded and not functioning", "toilets disgusting", "felt very unsafe", "packed up within 15 minutes and fled", "great location", "pool was closed", "facilities very run down", "would never go there again", "close to Swan Valley attractions"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of family entertainment facilities, kids activities, or playground equipment mentioned across all reviews Strongly negative sentiment from at least one reviewer with no explanation or counterbalance from family-specific positive experiences Serious connectivity issues with 'shockingly bad phone reception' and no evidence of location advantages or proximity to family attractions
rationale_honourable: Some positive mentions of natural surroundings with descriptions like 'nice place' and 'private park next to heaven', though specific nature features remain undocumented
water_fun: No pool or water facilities mentioned
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment mentioned
best_for: Adults seeking a peaceful, private stopover location rather than families with young children needing activities and entertainment

PARK: Wanneroo Caravan Park

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (0/20) — No mention of any kids' activities, playground equipment, jumping pillow, games room, or organized entertainment in any reviews.
Nature (10/20) — Described as a "nice place" with reference to being "next to heaven" suggesting some natural appeal, but no specific details about bushland, wildlife, or nature experiences.
Cleanliness (8/15) — Park appears adequately maintained with "trimmed" grounds mentioned, though limited detail provided about facilities cleanliness.
Value (5/15) — No pricing or value commentary in reviews, making it impossible to assess affordability for families.
Site Size (5/10) — Described as "private park" suggesting smaller scale, but no specific feedback about site spaciousness or suitability for large family setups.
Sentiment (5/10) — Mixed reviews with one strongly negative "Did not like it at all" balanced by positive ratings, averaging 4.25 stars but limited family-specific enthusiasm.
Location (2/10) — "Shockingly bad phone reception" indicates remote or poorly serviced location with no mentions of proximity to family attractions, beaches, or conveniences.

WHY NOT 100:

2. Very poor mobile phone reception documented, making it difficult for families to stay connected or access emergency services.
3. One review explicitly stated dissatisfaction with no explanation, and no reviews mention family-specific experiences, water play, playgrounds, or kid-friendly features.

rationale_top3: Severe cleanliness and hygiene failures with families reporting disgusting bathrooms, cockroaches, mould, filthy floors and amenities not cleaned for entire stays - creating unsafe conditions especially for young children. Critical safety concerns including broken concrete causing trip hazards, poor lighting at night, non-functioning fire alarms in cabins, and families feeling unsafe enough to not leave accommodation after dark. Children's facilities completely inadequate with playground closed for renovations, games room closed, tennis courts unusable, and no organised kids activities or entertainment - the opposite of what family holiday parks should provide.
rationale_honourable: Positive aspects include large concrete slabbed sites for caravans and convenient location within walking distance to shops and public transport, though these are significantly outweighed by critical safety and cleanliness concerns.
water_fun: Pool generally clean but limited facilities

best_for: This park is best suited for budget travellers or workers needing temporary Perth accommodation with their own van, not families seeking a holiday experience.
executive_summary:
PARK: Acclaim Kingsway Tourist Park
SCORE: 32/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 130

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews consistently report playground closed for renovations, games room closed, tennis courts unusable and "cracked covered in weeds", basketball court "below average". One family noted "expectations there would be a lot for the kids to do" but found facilities unavailable. The only positive is the pool, with bees and wasps reported around it.

Nature (8/20) — Limited natural environment mentioned. Reviews note "giant gum trees" and "love hearing the birds, especially the kookaburras". One review mentions "nature reserve walk trail across the road". However, grounds described as "overgrown", "trees need trimming badly", and "not much gardening done". Location is 15 minutes to beach and 20 minutes to CBD.

Cleanliness (2/15) — Overwhelmingly negative feedback. Multiple families report "dirtiest caravan park", "absolutely disgusting inside and out", "toilets and showers were disgusting" with "hair and sand throughout", "not cleaned once during our 8 day stay". Specific reports include "cockroach crawling on me", "dead or alive cockroach in the shared fridge", "bathroom shower had so much grime", "floors were so filthy my socks were black", "mould, rotting wood and mildew". While some recent reviews mention new management improving cleanliness, the majority of family experiences were extremely poor.

Value (6/15) — Mixed feedback on value. Some praise "great value for money" and "very affordable" with "stay 7 pay 6 deal". However, multiple families found it "overpriced" at "$50 a night for an unpowered site" given the condition, with one stating "can't believe the prices they ask for with it looking the state it's in". The consensus is you get budget pricing but facilities don't match even that level.

Site Size (7/10) — Positive feedback on "large concrete sites", "huge slabbed sites", "nice large site", and "spacious sites". Several reviews mention sites are adequate with "plenty of parking". However, issues noted with "broken concrete pavers everywhere", "caravan pad was broken", and some permanent resident areas being messy.

Sentiment (2/10) — Extremely poor family sentiment. Multiple reviews from families state "would never stay again", "do not waste your time", "worst caravan park we have ever stayed in", "avoid at all costs", "we cut our stay short", "didn't stay our two nights we had booked". One family specifically noted "we are a family of four and did not go out after the sunset" due to safety concerns. Very few families would recommend or return.

Location (8/10) — Strong positive aspect. Reviews consistently praise "great location", "close to everything", "walking distance to shops", "shopping centre 10 minutes walk away", "5 mins from train station and bus stop right outside", "cycling path into perth was superb". Location rated as convenient for Perth CBD access and northern beaches.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Severe cleanliness and maintenance failures make this unsafe and unsuitable for families with young children, with multiple reports of uncleaned facilities, pests, broken amenities, and safety hazards like broken concrete and poor lighting.
2. Complete lack of functional children's entertainment facilities, with playground closed, games room closed, tennis courts unusable, and only a poor basketball court available, leaving families with nothing for kids to do.
3. Overwhelmingly negative family sentiment with multiple reports of families cutting stays short, feeling unsafe, and experiencing accommodation that was "inhabitable" with cockroaches, mould, sewage smells, and filth throughout cabins and facilities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"dirtiest caravan park we have done after 2 weeks around Western Australia", "family of four did not go out after the sunset", "toilets and showers were disgusting, not cleaned once during our 8 day stay", "cockroach crawling on me in the middle of the night", "playground was closed, games room closed, tennis courts run down", "absolutely a dump, broken concrete pavers everywhere", "should not be open to the public", "new management are fantastic, trying hard to improve"
